source.book: 《9分达人雅思听力真题还原及解析7》

segments.text: Troy: Hey Gabriella, how is it going?
segments.translation: 嘿，加布里埃拉，最近怎么样？

segments.text: Gabriella: I've been pretty busy lately.
segments.translation: 我最近一直很忙。

segments.text: Troy: Oh, what are you up to?
segments.translation: 哦，你在忙什么？

segments.text: Gabriella: I've been working on a dissertation on music education specializing in music performance.
segments.translation: 我一直在写一篇关于音乐教育的学位论文，研究方向是音乐表演。

segments.text: Troy: Sounds intriguing.
segments.translation: 听起来很有意思。

segments.text: Troy: What have you found?
segments.translation: 你有什么发现？

segments.text: Gabriella: Well, I interviewed 18 freshmen majoring in music performance as my sample population.
segments.translation: 我选取了18名音乐表演专业的大一新生作为样本，并对他们进行了访谈。

segments.text: Gabriella: I first asked about their music background before being accepted to music schools.
segments.translation: 我首先询问了他们在被音乐学院录取前的音乐背景。

segments.text: Troy: Let me guess, have they all got relevant qualifications in music?
segments.translation: 让我猜猜，他们是不是都取得了相关的音乐资格证书？

segments.text: Gabriella: Well, even though there is a wealth of music qualifications available, most of the students didn't receive any until they entered music schools.
segments.translation: 虽然可供选择的音乐资格证书很多，但大多数学生进入音乐学院后才获得证书。

segments.text: Gabriella: Some capabilities like music production or music technology are pretty tough going.
segments.translation: 音乐制作或音乐技术等技能学起来相当吃力。

segments.text: Troy: Now that you've mentioned it, I recall that I got mine in sound engineering in my sophomore year.
segments.translation: 听你这么一说，我想起自己大二时拿到了音响工程方面的资格证书。

segments.text: Gabriella: Right.
segments.translation: 对。

segments.text: Gabriella: But the majority of these students admitted that they had performed in front of a large crowd.
segments.translation: 不过，大多数学生承认自己曾在一大群观众面前表演过。

segments.text: Gabriella: Around 80% of them had performed on stage as band members in secondary schools.
segments.translation: 其中约80%的人中学时曾作为乐队成员登台演出。

segments.text: Gabriella: A few of them played piano or guitar at the time.
segments.translation: 当时有几个人会弹钢琴或吉他。

segments.text: Gabriella: And it also surprised me that they were generally able to play multiple instruments with proficiency.
segments.translation: 同样令我惊讶的是，他们通常都能熟练演奏多种乐器。

segments.text: Troy: Have they tried to compose a piece of music before?
segments.translation: 他们以前尝试过作曲吗？

segments.text: Gabriella: Only in rare cases did they have prior experience in composition.
segments.translation: 只有极少数人以前有过作曲经验。

segments.text: Troy: They would have to do it sooner or later.
segments.translation: 他们迟早都得尝试。

segments.text: Troy: There is a whole module for it.
segments.translation: 有一整个课程模块都是关于作曲的。

segments.text: Troy: What did they say about practising?
segments.translation: 他们对练习有什么看法？

segments.text: Gabriella: All subjects mentioned that they feared being marked too low and tried their best.
segments.translation: 所有受访者都说自己担心分数太低，因此会竭尽全力。

segments.text: Troy: So they were like practising every single day?
segments.translation: 所以他们每天都练习吗？

segments.text: Gabriella: Not for most people.
segments.translation: 大多数人并非如此。

segments.text: Gabriella: Some students worked with others in a band, so they had to arrange the time for rehearsals.
segments.translation: 有些学生和其他人一起组乐队，所以必须安排排练时间。

segments.text: Gabriella: But all 18 students had sufficient amount of practice.
segments.translation: 不过，这18名学生的练习量都很充足。

segments.text: Gabriella: And they frequently mentioned that playing technical pieces was exhilarating.
segments.translation: 他们还经常提到，演奏技巧难度高的曲目令人兴奋。

segments.text: Troy: I feel that way, too.
segments.translation: 我也有这种感觉。

segments.text: Gabriella: And then I asked questions about how they chose the pieces that were assessed and their attempts to improve their performance in recital classes.
segments.translation: 然后，我询问了他们如何选择考核曲目，以及如何努力提高自己在独奏课上的表现。

segments.text: Troy: What did they say?
segments.translation: 他们怎么说？

segments.text: Gabriella: Two students mentioned that they always asked their tutors' advice on the pieces they were going to perform because they felt that would give them a competitive edge over those who had no clue of tutors' preference in music.
segments.translation: 两名学生提到，他们总会就准备演奏的曲目征求导师的意见，因为他们觉得，与不了解导师音乐偏好的学生相比，这样做能让自己更具竞争优势。

segments.text: Troy: Well, that's a bit sneaky.
segments.translation: 嗯，这有点投机取巧。

segments.text: Gabriella: But the most common remark was that they had a minimal interest in pieces that the public barely knew.
segments.translation: 不过，最常见的说法是，他们对公众几乎不了解的曲目兴趣很小。

segments.text: Gabriella: Instead, most prefer popular pieces that other students would also choose.
segments.translation: 相反，大多数人更喜欢其他学生也会选择的热门曲目。

segments.text: Troy: How come?
segments.translation: 为什么呢？

segments.text: Gabriella: They said that this could mirror their weaknesses and thus help them to perfect the techniques for handling the most difficult bits.
segments.translation: 他们说，这样可以反映出自己的弱点，从而帮助他们完善处理最难部分的技巧。

segments.text: Gabriella: All but one of the 18 students admitted that to perform well, they had to learn the music by heart, or otherwise, they would have to divert their attention and thus make some errors.
segments.translation: 18名学生中除一人外，其他人都承认，要想表现出色就必须把乐曲背熟，否则就不得不分散注意力，进而犯下一些错误。

segments.text: Troy: I agree.
segments.translation: 我同意。

segments.text: Gabriella: Then I asked them about their thoughts on practising in different aspects.
segments.translation: 接着，我询问了他们对不同练习方面的看法。

segments.text: Gabriella: And some of their answers were entirely unexpected.
segments.translation: 他们的一些回答完全出乎我的意料。

segments.text: Troy: Oh, what aspects did you look into?
segments.translation: 哦，你研究了哪些方面？

segments.text: Gabriella: I first asked how they feel about playing solo.
segments.translation: 我先问了他们对独奏的感受。

segments.text: Troy: What did they say?
segments.translation: 他们怎么说？

segments.text: Gabriella: They said being a solo performer takes desire, focus and hard work.
segments.translation: 他们说，要成为独奏者，需要热情、专注和努力。

segments.text: Gabriella: And the performance would not pull off if one took the stage without confidence.
segments.translation: 如果一个人没有自信就登台，演出就不会成功。

segments.text: Gabriella: But they all took great pleasure in it.
segments.translation: 但他们都从独奏中获得了极大的乐趣。

segments.text: Troy: Yeah, that's quite different from playing in an orchestra, where musicians have to work with other members to produce a harmonious sound that is pleasing to the ear.
segments.translation: 是啊，这和在管弦乐团中演奏很不一样；在乐团里，乐手必须与其他成员合作，奏出悦耳和谐的声音。

segments.text: Gabriella: Right.
segments.translation: 没错。

segments.text: Gabriella: And I also asked whether the choice of the piece to play would affect their performance.
segments.translation: 我还问了曲目的选择是否会影响他们的表现。

segments.text: Troy: Well, that's a big yes for me.
segments.translation: 嗯，对我来说肯定会。

segments.text: Troy: When I play a song I don't like, I won't devote myself to it.
segments.translation: 演奏自己不喜欢的曲子时，我不会全身心投入。

segments.text: Gabriella: That's widely accepted.
segments.translation: 大家普遍认同这一点。

segments.text: Gabriella: The audience can tell when performers are being authentic and inspired by the music they are performing.
segments.translation: 观众能分辨出演奏者是否流露真情，以及是否受到所演奏音乐的感染。

segments.text: Gabriella: Many would spend hours or even days choosing the right piece.
segments.translation: 许多人会花上数小时甚至数天来挑选合适的曲目。

segments.text: Troy: You have to put a lot of thought into it before it's too late.
segments.translation: 你必须在为时已晚之前仔细考虑。

segments.text: Troy: So what kind of musical genre do they prefer?
segments.translation: 那么，他们更喜欢哪种音乐类型？

segments.text: Gabriella: The answers vary vastly.
segments.translation: 他们的答案差异很大。

segments.text: Gabriella: But one thing in common is that they rarely switch from one style to another.
segments.translation: 但有一点是共同的，那就是他们很少从一种风格转向另一种风格。

segments.text: Troy: Of course.
segments.translation: 当然。

segments.text: Troy: It is tough to make such a transition.
segments.translation: 完成这种转变很难。

segments.text: Troy: Most instrumentalists initially start with some classical based training.
segments.translation: 大多数器乐演奏者起初都接受过一些以古典音乐为基础的训练。

segments.text: Troy: But they don't know how to take their already existing knowledge and apply it to a new style of music.
segments.translation: 但他们不知道如何把已有的知识运用到一种新的音乐风格中。

segments.text: Troy: What about their feedback on playing duets?
segments.translation: 他们对二重奏有什么看法？

segments.text: Gabriella: Yes, that's the last question on my list.
segments.translation: 对，这是我问题清单上的最后一个问题。

segments.text: Gabriella: I asked their take on playing a duet with someone with the same musical instrument, like a violin duet.
segments.translation: 我询问了他们对两人使用相同乐器演奏二重奏的看法，比如小提琴二重奏。

segments.text: Gabriella: Although it is pretty demanding to do so, they all admitted that it is helpful in that it renews their motivation while enhancing their technical skills.
segments.translation: 尽管这样做要求很高，但他们都承认，这有助于重新激发动力，同时提升演奏技巧。

segments.text: Troy: So, have you started writing the 1st draft of your dissertation?
segments.translation: 那么，你开始写论文初稿了吗？

segments.text: Troy: I can help if...
segments.translation: 如果需要的话，我可以帮忙……
